content.problemStatement:
You are given a string **S** of length **N** consisting only of **0**s and **1**s. You are also given an integer **K**.

You have to answer **Q** queries. In the **i**th query, two integers **Li** and **Ri** are given. Then you should print the number of substrings of **S[L, R]** which contain at most **K** **0**s and at most **K** **1**s where **S[L, R]** denotes the substring from **L**th to **R**th characters of the string **S**. 
In other words, you have to count number of pairs **(i, j)** of integers such that **L ≤ i ≤ j ≤ R** such that no character in substring **S[i, j]** occurs more than **K** times.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases in one test file does not exceed 10^5
Sum of Q over all test cases in one test file does not exceed 10^5
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains three space-separated integers N, K, and Q on the first line, followed by a string S of length N on the second line. Then, Q lines follow, each containing two space-separated integers L and R.
content.outputFormat: For each query, print the required answer in a single line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Explanation: Query 1: Consider substring P = S[1, 4] = "0111". Out of 10 total substrings of P, substrings P[1, 4] and P[2, 4] are not valid because both contain more than two 1s. Other substrings contains at most two 0s and at most two 1s, thus the answer is 8.

Explanation: Query 1: Consider substring P = S[1, 3] = "101". Out of 6 total substrings of P, substrings P[1, 3] is not valid because it contains more than one 1. Other substrings contains at most one 0 and at most one 1, thus the answer is 4.
Explanation: Query 1: Consider substring P = S[1, 10] = "1111000000". Out of 55 total substrings of P, all substrings contains at most three 0s and at most three 1s, thus the answer is 55.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved by iterating over all possible substrings of S[L, R] and counting the number of substrings that contain at most K 0s and at most K 1s. This can be achieved by using two nested loops to generate all possible substrings and then checking the count of 0s and 1s in each substring.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N^3) per test case, O(T * N^3) total. The precomputation is done once for each test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — the dominant structure is the input string S.
